title: Trump’s War in Iran, and Rising Gas Prices, Collide With Midterm Agenda
summary: The attack on Iran has led to a surge in energy prices at a moment when the cost of living is a major issue heading into the fall elections.

title: New Supreme Leader Inherits Sprawling, Secretive Office That Dominates Iran
summary: His father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, had turned what was traditionally a religious affairs office into a shadowy national security juggernaut.

title: War Brings New Water Crises to an Already-Parched Iran
summary: Iran has accused the United States of bombing a desalination plant on Qeshm Island. The country was already facing a severe water shortage.

title: Trump Zigzags on When War Will End, and Investigators Search Epstein’s Ranch in New Mexico
summary: Plus, space mirrors?

title: U.S. Solar Installations Fell in 2025 as Trump Attacked Clean Energy
summary: More solar energy was added to U.S. grids than any other technology, but the amount installed fell by 14 percent, according to a new report.

title: Two Supreme Court Justices Debate Handling of Trump Emergency Cases
summary: In a rare joint appearance, Justices Ketanji Brown Jackson and Brett M. Kavanaugh offered sharply different views on how the court should handle emergency requests.

title: Trump Antisemitism Inquiry Demanding List of Jews at Penn Heads to Court
summary: The Trump administration, which said it is investigating harassment, sued the University of Pennsylvania after it refused a request to provide information about Jewish students and staff.

title: Alexander Brothers Found Guilty on All Counts in Sex-Trafficking Trial
summary: The verdict comes more than a month after the trial began in Federal District Court in Manhattan where the jury heard weeks of emotional and often graphic testimony.

title: Casting, Which Will Be Celebrated at This Year’s Oscars, Has Changed Drastically
summary: What once involved an actor and a casting director in a room is now a technologically advanced exercise with pros and cons for performers.

title: How Best Picture is Decided
summary: Best picture is the only Oscar category decided by preferential ballot. Our reporter Marc Tracy explains how that works.

title: Here Are the Key Races to Watch in Mississippi and Georgia Elections
summary: It’s primary day in Mississippi, where a younger Democrat is trying to oust a House veteran, and in Georgia, where Marjorie Taylor Greene’s seat is up for grabs.

title: Democrats Sued to Find Out Whether Trump Will Send Armed Officers to Election Sites
summary: In a lawsuit filed Tuesday, the D.N.C. sought to compel the government to say whether it plans to deploy armed federal officers in this year’s elections.

title: iPads in Kindergarten, YouTube on Breaks: The School Screen-Time Battle
summary: Mounting evidence shows that excessive computer use can harm children, so parents are cutting back at home. Now, the debate has shifted to the classroom.

title: Mamdani Chooses His Words Carefully After Alleged Terror Attack
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has been spare in his remarks following the attempted bombing at Gracie Mansion and arrests of two men who said they were motivated by ISIS.

title: Cancer Haunts Neighbors of Canada’s Oil Sands Wastelands
summary: Though high rates of the disease persist among the nearby Indigenous communities, the Canadian government is weighing rules that may allow energy giants to release treated mining waste into the river system.

title: Who Is Balendra Shah, the Rapper Set to Be Nepal’s Next Prime Minister?
summary: Balendra Shah’s party won a landslide in the election that followed Nepal’s Gen Z revolution. His style is pugnacious.

title: I Asked a Former Trump Official to Justify This War
summary: Nadia Schadlow, a deputy national security adviser during Trump’s first term, explains how the U.S.-Israel war with Iran fits in with an “America First” agenda.

title: Trump Has No Idea How to End the War With Iran
summary: Only when the U.S.-Israeli bombing of Iran comes to a halt, without destroying the state, can the Iranian people sort out their fate.

title: War and the Economy
summary: We look at the conflict’s global economic impact.

title: Late Night Scolds Trump Over Gasoline Prices
summary: “You can lie about many things in American life, but one thing you can’t lie about is gas prices,” Late Night host Seth Meyers said. “Everyone sees it.”

title: Fiscal Crunch Presents a First Big Policy Challenge for Mikie Sherrill
summary: Ms. Sherrill will present her first budget as New Jersey governor in Trenton on Tuesday, and with it a robust preview of her policy priorities.

title: Epstein’s Remote Zorro Ranch Is Searched by New Mexico Investigators
summary: Some of the disgraced financier’s victims have said they were trafficked at the property south of Santa Fe, where the nearest neighbors are miles away.

title: Bo Gritz, Vietnam Veteran Called a Real-Life Rambo, Dies at 87
summary: He served in the Special Forces, led a postwar raid to find P.O.W.s and became a voice of the right-wing anti-government fringe.

title: Meteorite Crashes Through Roof in Germany After Fiery Light Show
summary: The fireball from space was spotted by a network of sky-watching cameras in Belgium, France, Luxembourg, the Netherlands and Germany.
